perturbation.original_text: Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue indefinitely, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term from 'three (3) years' to 'indefinitely,' a contradiction is introduced. The contract initially specifies a fixed duration but is subsequently changed to an indefinite duration, creating confusion on when the agreement expires. This creates an in-text contradiction in the 'Term' definition itself, making the duration of the contract unclear and subject to dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue indefinitely, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a term of three years, while the changed text specifies an indefinite term. The 'Term' section itself is the site of the contradiction. Having two different durations for the same agreement creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction, because one duration is finite and the other is not. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: DeltaThree shall be responsible for all costs and expenses in connection with the operation and maintenance of the PrimeCall Web Site and the services provided pursuant to this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] shall be provided at no additional cost to PrimeCall; provided, however, that PrimeCall shall be fully responsible for all costs associated with the purchase of hardware, software and domain names related to the PrimeCall website.
perturbation.changed_text: DeltaThree shall be responsible for all costs and expenses in connection with the operation and maintenance of the PrimeCall Web Site and the services provided pursuant to this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] shall be provided at no additional cost to PrimeCall; provided, however, that PrimeCall shall be responsible for a portion of the costs associated with the purchase of hardware, software and domain names related to the PrimeCall website, as determined by DeltaThree's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 4.01. The original clause states that DeltaThree covers costs and expenses for the PrimeCall website, while PrimeCall is responsible for the purchase of hardware, software, and domain names related to the PrimeCall website. The altered version makes PrimeCall responsible for a 'portion' of these same costs, with the 'portion' being at DeltaThree's discretion. This directly contradicts the initial statement that DeltaThree would cover all other operational costs, while making the expenses portion for PrimeCall to pay is not constant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services]. Web Site Development and Services. Within three (3) months of the date hereof, DeltaThree shall establish and administrate a PrimeCall web site (the "PrimeCall Web Site") in consultation with PrimeCall which will enable PrimeCall to market and sell its calling cards via the World Wide Web and provide on-line support for such sales. The PrimeCall Web Site shall be a fully functional e-commerce site, with all ordering and billing performed on-line. DeltaThree shall provide ongoing upgrades to the PrimeCall Web Site, shall develop a database for the PrimeCall Web Site, and shall oversee all billing, collections and fulfillment for on-line orders. PrimeCall shall establish its own merchant account with Citibank or another accredited United States banking institution and provide DeltaThree with all information pertaining to such account to enable DeltaThree to process all on-line credit card transactions for PrimeCall. PrimeCall shall be fully liable for all charge-backs, refunds and commission payments associated with the processing of PrimeCall's on-line credit card transactions by DeltaThree. DeltaThree shall establish for PrimeCall an on-line interactive center whereby PrimeCall shall be able to monitor the on-line sales of its calling cards. All calling card and pricing information shall be 3 provided by PrimeCall. DeltaThree shall be responsible for all costs and expenses in connection with the operation and maintenance of the PrimeCall Web Site and the services provided pursuant to this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] shall be provided at no additional cost to PrimeCall; provided, however, that PrimeCall shall be responsible for a portion of the costs associated with the purchase of hardware, software and domain names related to the PrimeCall website, as determined by DeltaThree's discretion. PrimeCall agrees to cooperate and provide necessary support to assist DeltaThree in providing the services under this Section 4.01 [Web Site Development and Services] and shall be responsible for supplying DeltaThree with the content and graphics (look and feel) of the PrimeCall website. In addition, upon the execution of this Agreement, each party shall appoint an account manager to work directly on the implementation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters PrimeCall's responsibility for hardware, software, and domain name costs. The original text states PrimeCall is fully responsible. The changed text makes PrimeCall responsible for a 'portion' of those costs, determined by DeltaThree. The contradicted text initially sets the expectation of PrimeCall's 'full' responsibility, now making them responsible for a portion of the expenses, determined by DeltaThree. This creates a contradiction within Section 4.01, specifically the last sentence of the section, making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 9.09 [Assignment]. Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall or in connection with a merger or consolidation of PrimeCall or a sale of all or substantially all of PrimeCall's business. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 9.09 [Assignment]. Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall; however, PrimeCall shall not assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces a direct self-contradiction in the section on assignment. The original section stated that PrimeCall *can* assign the agreement to an affiliate. The inserted phrase states that PrimeCall *cannot* assign the agreement to an affiliate, creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the same paragraph. This creates uncertainty about the assignment rights of PrimeCall and could lead to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9.09 [Assignment]. Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may not be assigned by DeltaThree without the prior written consent of PrimeCall. PrimeCall may assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall; however, PrimeCall shall not assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall. Except as provided in the preceding sentence, this Agreement may not be assigned by PrimeCall without the prior written consent of DeltaThree.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allows PrimeCall to assign the agreement to an affiliate. The changed text adds 'however, PrimeCall shall not assign this Agreement to any affiliate of PrimeCall', directly negating the previous statement. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict within the same section regarding PrimeCall's assignment rights, satisfying the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.
